title: 第 31 節
en: Trita is clearly a representative of Agni, as Agni is of Surya, while in some of his acts he takes the place of Indra. Like Agni, Trita has to fight VWtra and is assisted in his battle by the Maruts, who are known as the constant helpers of Indra in the same battle. Trita, like Indra, is said to have cleft the defences of Vala. When hidden in the waters, Trita was said to have been inside the vavra, the very name given elsewhere to the hidingplace of the cows, the cows of Tvashfra (Visvarupa), which Trita had to rescue (R,v. X, 8, 8), or which Indra rescued for him (Rv. X, 48, 2). Here we see again how easily the Vedic gods exchange places, and how they perform the same work, only under different names. The hiding-place of Trita, the vavra, is really the same as the anarambhanam tamas, the endless darkness from which the light and some of its legendary representatives such as Atri, Vandana and others emerge every day. Sometimes vavra occurs in the plural as the endless hiding-places (vavran anantan, Rv. VII, 104, 17) into which evil spirits are to fall. Trita's home and character appear very clearly when it is explained (Rv. I, 163, 2) how the gods made a horse out of the sun, that is, represented the sun as a Vl] TRITA AND TRITA. 667 horse. At that time Yama, it is said, the most distant deity, gave the horse ; the next in place, Trita, harnessed it, and Indra was the first to bestride it. Sometimes this solar horse is actually identified with Yama and Trita (Rv. I, 163, 3) ; or it is said that Trita, standing, as it were, behind the rising sun, blows like a smith with his bellows upon the rising Agni (Rv. V, 9, 5). It is said of the same Agni that he was found by Trita (i. e. in the far distance) before he, Agni, whether fire or sun, became by his light the friend of all householders (Rv. X, 46, 3).

The difficult part of the Trita myth is his fight with Tvashfra Visvarupa. Before we can attempt to explain this we must remember that Trita before his fight drinks Soma, and likewise provides Soma to impart strength to Indra for his daily fight. The underlying idea seems to have been that Tvash^H, who was the father of Saranyu, the earliest dawn, was also the father of the moon, called trisiras, three-headed (the waxing, the full, and the waning moon), saptarasmi, seven-rayed (the sennight), and shac/aksha, six-eyed (seasons ?)x. This demon was slain by Indra as well as by Trita, and thus the Soma or Amrita in the moon was set free. That Tvashfra, the son of Tva,shtri, was meant for the moon is indicated by the epithet visvarupa, assuming all forms, i. e. constantly changing, and as such a proper epithet of the constantly changing moon. Rv. VI, 41,3: EshaA drapsaA vn'shabhaA visvarupaA, indraya vr/srme sam akari s6maA, ' The strong multi- 1 The seasons are sometimes called the brothers of Soma, cf. Hillebrandt, p. 298.
VOL. II. R 668 TRITA AND TBITA. [chap.
form drop, Soma, was prepared for the strong Indra.' Tvash^W himself also is called visvarupa (Rv. Ill, 55, 19), and he is said to have created all forms and all things.

If, therefore, the moon or Visvarupa held the Soma, the rain or the refreshing dew of the night \ it was but natural that Trita or any other solar deity should, in order to gain the invigorating Soma, fight him or cut off his three heads. In this way we gain some kind of meaning in the destruction of Trisiras Visvarupa, the son of Tv&shtri, by Trita or by Indra. Whether this is the exact meaning which was in the minds of the poets of the Rig-veda or the writers of the Brahmawas, I should not venture to assert positively, but it is at all events the meaning at which another scholar, Prof. Hillebrandt, arrived independently in his Vedische Mythologie, p. 53 seq. No better interpretation has as yet been suggested, and till that is done, it may be allowed to stand.

If, then, we saw in Trita a derivative of one root tar, to go through, older than tri, and if that conception of the transit of the heavenly bodies from East to West found constant expression in the Veda, the question is whether it has left some traces in Greek mythology also.
Trita in Greek Mythology.

We saw that Trita was supposed to dwell in the most distant region from whence rose the sun, or the solar horse. This is to all intents and purposes the place which the Greeks meant by Tartaros. Tartaros, however, was originally, like Hades, not 1 Yad ardram tat saumyam, vatrih saumya. Taitt. Samh. I, 6, 3, 23. Hillebrandt, p. 459.

Vl] TRITA IN GREEK MYTHOLOGY. 669 the name of a place, but of a person, the son of Aither and Ge, himself father of Typhoeus, Echidna, and the Gigantes. Homer always uses Hades as a personal, not yet as a local name. We can watch the same transition in the case of Trita, for though originally clearly a person, we can see how in certain passages Trita also assumes a local character. In Ev. IX, 37, 4, we read: SaA Tritdsya ddhi sanavi, ' (he lighted up the sun) on the top of Trita.'

Tartara would be a reduplicated form of the same root that yielded Trita, or rather Trita, for which we expect in Greek Tapros, and would originally have meant he who constantly goes beyond or sets in the sea or in the place where the nocturnal sun after its setting was supposed to dwell, the place of Yama, himself originally the setting sun, sometimes spoken of as the first of those who died.

Benfey has shown l, that Tartaros is a parallel form of Talatala, which means the lower world in Sanskrit, but he takes it in the general sense of what is below, the descent, or the bottom (tala). He has removed all formal objections to the equation raprapos = talatala, but he did not perceive the mythological connection between Trita and rdprap-oq. The intensive of tri is tatariti and tatarti.

Having now cleared up the meaning of Trita and his close relation with the lower world in the waters (samudre) or under the waters, I feel more inclined than formerly to accept the equation Trita (Aptya) = Triton. Of course the Greek i in Triton is long, and therefore the equation is irregular, but so is Tnta from tri, and Trita from tri, three, and yet both 1 Hermes, Minos und Tartaros, 1877. R 2 670 TRITA IN GREEK MYTHOLOGY. [chap.

are real forms. If the irregularity in Sanskrit is due to an attempt to supply a clearer meaning to TWta, could not the length in Greek Triton be due to the length of the i in Tritogeneia ? This was a very common and popular name, and if the i had once been lengthened in it, in order to make the name possible in epic poetry, it would become almost a necessity to speak of Triton, a double perversion of TWta. The change of TWta into Trita would then seem to be previous not only to the IndoIranian, but even to the Aryan Separation. The derivative suffix in Triton differs as that of Ploutos and Plouton, but if Triton is a son of Poseidon and Amphitrite (or Kelaino), if he with the other Tritons lives with his father on the bottom of the sea, this would agree with the true Trita, nay it would help us to understand why the same name occurs in different places in Greece, that is, wherever the sun seemed to descend, and dark surroundings had suggested an entrance into the lower regions of the earth. The names of Athene, as Tritogeneia, Trlto, Tritonia, and Tritonis, might thus receive new light, for if the sun was supposed in the Veda to rise from the top of TWta, the goddess of the dawn could not' claim a truer birthplace.

We need hardly refer to the old derivations of Tritogeneia from the Kretan word rpua) which is said to have meant head, or from the Boeotian river Triton, which falls into the Lake of Kopais, near the Pelasgian city of Athenai. Both derivations would give to the name of Tritogeneia too local and too narrow a character, and they both leave the impression of being scholastic interpretations of a name that had become unintelligible. In later times Vl] TRITA IN GREEK MYTHOLOGY. 67 1 wherever there was a river Triton, the people naturally claimed Tritonia or Tritogeneia as their goddess.

One more step remains. We saw that Manu Vaivasvata was mentioned in conjunction with Trita (Rv. VIII, 52, 1) and with Yama, the lord of the departed. That Manu is the Sanskrit form to which Minos corresponds in Greek can no longer be doubted after the explanation given by Kuhn and afterwards by Benfey \ We thus arrive at an important conclusion, namely that before the Aryan Separation took place, not only had a belief grown up in an underworld, but one person at least had been named whose duty it was to represent it, Manu among the ancestors of the Indians, Minos among the ancestors of the Greeks.

I suggested some time ago that the idea of a third god, a Trita, had been reached before the Teutonic and Indian branches separated. I thought this could be admitted on the strength of the Old Norse Thridi, a name of Odinn, as the mate of Har, and Jafnhar. Grimm's Tweggi 2, however, is very doubtful, and could not represent Dvita. If we accept Trita in Sanskrit as a misapprehension of TWta, we should have to admit that this misapprehension took place before the Aryan Separation. But the admission of three characters as the representatives of the sun as morning, noon, and night, is so natural that we cannot deny the possibility of its having arisen independently among different branches of the Aryan family. On the other hand, we can hardly doubt that the misapprehension took place before the 1 Hermes, Minos und Tartaros, pp. 11-14.
2 Germ. Mythol., p. 149.
672 TRITA IN GREEK MYTHOLOGY. [chap.

Separation of the Aryas of India and Persia, for the Avestic form Thraetaona presupposes a form derived from Trita, such as Traitana (Rv. I, 158, 5), not from Trita. Besides, in the Avesta Thrita is constantly spoken of as the third of Soma-priests, as the most beneficial of the Samas, the third mortal who prepared Soma for the mortal world, &c.

It was probably the most brilliant discovery ever made in Comparative Mythology that the name of the great hero of the Persian epic, the Shahnameh, Feridun, is a lineal descendant of the Sk. Trita and Traitana, that the Vedic Trita Aptya appears in the Avesta as Thraetaona, son of Athwya. It is extraordinary that this discovery of Burnouf's should again and again have been ascribed to Roth, who, I believe, would have been the first to protest against it. The identity of Vivasvat and Viva?ihvat was discovered by Bopp, but it was Burnouf who traced the Persian Feridun back to Thraetaona, and the Persian Gershasb, the Avestic Keresaspa, back to the Vedic KWsasva. It was from 1840 to 1846 that Burnouf published in the Journal Asiatique his ' Etudes sur la Langue et les Textes Zends,' and showed for the first time how the three epic heroes of Persia, Jemshid, Feridun, and Gershasb descended from Yima Ksha§ta, Thraetaona, and Keresaspa, the Avestic representatives of the three earliest generations of mankind, whose more distant prototypes again can be discovered in the Vedic Yama, Trita, and KWsasva. He showed further that as Vivasvat was the father of Yama in the Veda, Vivanhvat was the father of Yima in the Avesta, and that as Trita is called Aptya in the Veda, Thraetaona in Persia is the son of Athwya. Zohak, the Persian Vl] TRITA IN GREEK MYTHOLOGY. 673 tyrant who was slain by Feridun , was likewise traced back to the Avestic Azhi dahaka, the serpent, the Ahi of the Veda. These brilliant discoveries were important, not only as showing the intimate relation between the Veda, the Avesta, and the Shahnameh (iooo a.d.), but as a palpable illustration of the transition of ancient mythology into epic poetry, and lastly into so-called history, a transition which has been so lightly set aside as incredible, when it was attempted to prove that the siege of Troy was a mythological and not a historical event in the strict sense of the word.
Hermes and Apollon.

If we accept Zeus as representing the god of the sky in his highest glory, as the vi//ictto? /xeyicrros of the gods, and if we leave the earth, the common realm of the three Kronidai, as represented chiefly by Demeter, Kore, Hestia, and other female agents, we have still to analyse some other gods, representing the sun at his rising and setting, or as the morning and evening sky, viz. Hermes and Apollon.
We have already examined several names of the Dawn ; but there is one more of great importance, not so much for the sake of the Dawn herself as for that of her son.

Hermes. Sarama. We have seen already that Sarama like Saranyu was originally meant for the Dawn, the names meaning the swift runner. This may seem a very unmeaning name for the Dawn. But we know by this time that nearly all names were in their beginning very general and, in that sense, unmeaning. It could not be otherwise. Thus from the root sar, to go, we 674 HERMES. SARAMA. [chap.

have not only Sarama, the swift goer, but likewise sara, waterfall, sarama, wind, saras, water, sarit, river, sarayu, wind, air, sari, spring, sarma, motion. All these words were general in the beginning, but they were narrowed down and made special by constant usage.

The name of Hermes, however, was not derived direct from the root sar, but from a derivative of that root, namely from Sarama. We saw, when examining the many stories of ' the Two with the One,' that her name had been carried along on the stream of mythology as that of the sister of the Dioskouroi, and afterwards as Helen of Troy. We must not imagine that Sarama in the Yeda and Helena in Homer are one and the same person. There would be no sense in that. All we have a right to say is that they both started from Sarama, used in the Veda as a name of the Dawn, or from something older still. But afterwards, when numerous stories came to be told of the Dawn, the ways of Greeks and Hindus parted once for all. Sarama, though she became the cause of war between IndA, and the Panis, never became the beautiful woman of whom Homer tells. Helena, after she had once been changed into the daughter of Zeus and Leda, the wife of Menelaos and the cause of the Trojan war, showed no longer any traces of her auroral or of her canine origin. In Greece we have more than one Helena, for she can hardly be called the same mythological person when carried off by Theseus and when carried off by Paris. All these stories, and probably many more, were told by the people of Argos, and what they had in common was no more than the far distant physical background of the Vl] HEKMES. SARAMA. 675 Dawn being loved and carried off by ever so many suitors, and being reconquered after a long fight between the powers of the East and the West, of Light and Darkness.

Sarama in the Veda is spoken of as a dog (not as a totem, however), but as a swift dog running along the sky that was supposed to find out the cows that had been carried off by the Pa/iis and hid in a cave. What is said of Ushas is said of Sarama, that she espied the strong stable of the cows, that she discovered the cleft of the rock, and that she led the cows out. But she is represented as doing all this, not of her own free will, but as the messenger of Indra. We hear in Greek mythology of a similar dog that belonged to Erigone, the early -born, but there is but little known of that dog, called Maira, and what she has to find is not the herd of cows, but the corpse of Erigone's father, Ikarios, whatever that legend may have been meant for.

The Vedic story tells us of a stable beyond the half real, half mythical river Rasa. Sarama had to cross that river in order to reach the cows. The stable is opened at last by Indra or BWhaspati, and the cows, that is, the rays of the morning, are released. This is only the old story of the morning cows told once more under a new form, and after the various versions of it which we have had before us, it need not detain us l at present.
Sarameyau.
What interests us at present is that Sarama is said to be the mother of one or two dogs, called Sarameya. It was Kuhn's merit to have discovered 1 Aufrecht, in Z. D. M. G. xiii, p. 493.
zh: 特利塔（Trita）明顯是阿格尼（Agni）的代表，如同阿格尼代表蘇利耶（Surya）一樣，而在某些行為中他則取代因陀羅（Indra）的地位。如同阿格尼一樣，特利塔必須與弗栗多（Vrtra）搏鬥，並在戰鬥中受到馬魯特（Marut）的協助，後者眾所周知是因陀羅在同一戰鬥中的常任助手。特利塔如同因陀羅一樣，據說曾經劈開瓦羅（Vala）的防禦。當隱沒於水中時，特利塔據說曾在瓦夫羅（vavra）內部，這正是在他處用來指稱牛隻藏匿處的名稱——特瓦什特里（Tvashtr）的牛隻（毗濕瓦魯帕），特利塔必須救出（《梨俱吠陀》X, 8, 8），或者因陀羅為他救出（《梨俱吠陀》X, 48, 2）。在此我們再次看到吠陀諸神多麼輕易地互換位置，以及他們如何執行同樣的工作，只是以不同的名稱。特利塔的藏匿處瓦夫羅，實質上就是無窮黑暗（anarambhanam tamas），光及其神話代表如阿特利（Atri）、旺達那（Vandana）等每日從其中現身的地方。有時瓦夫羅以複數形式出現，作為無盡的藏匿處（vavran anantan，《梨俱吠陀》VII, 104, 17），邪靈將墜入其中。當經文解釋諸神如何從太陽造出一匹馬（即將太陽表現為馬的形象）時（《梨俱吠陀》I, 163, 2），特利塔的家鄉與性格就極為清晰地呈現出來。當時據說死亡之神（Yama）乃最遙遠的提婆，給予了這匹馬；其次位階的特利塔套上了它，因陀羅則是第一個跨騎者。有時這匹太陽馬實際上就被認同為閻摩（Yama）與特利塔（《梨俱吠陀》I, 163, 3）；或者據說特利塔站立於上升太陽之後，如同鐵匠用風箱鼓吹般地吹向上升的阿格尼（《梨俱吠陀》V, 9, 5）。同一位阿格尼據說曾被特利塔（即在極遠之處）發現，在他——無論作為火或太陽——以其光芒成為所有家戶主人的友伴之前（《梨俱吠陀》X, 46, 3）。

特里塔神話中最困難的部分是他與特瓦什特拉‧毗濕瓦魯帕的戰鬥。在我們嘗試解釋這一點之前，必須記住特里塔在戰鬥前飲用蘇摩，同樣也為因陀羅提供蘇摩以增強其日常戰鬥的力量。潛在的觀念似乎是：特瓦什特拉是薩拉尼尤（最早的曙光）的父親，同時也是月亮的父親。月亮被稱為 trisiras（三頭：滿月前、滿月、滿月後），saptarasmi（七射：一週），shacaksha（六眼：季節？）。這個惡魔既被因陀羅擊殺，也被特里塔擊殺，因此月亮中的蘇摩或甘露得以解放。特瓦什特拉是特瓦什特里之子，旨在代表月亮，這由其上號詞毗濕瓦魯帕（具有一切形態，即不斷變化）所表明，這是對不斷變化之月亮的恰當稱號。梨俱吠陀第六卷第四十一讚第三節：「Esha drapsah vrishabhah visvarupah, indraya vritre sam akari somah」，意為「這強大的多變者……」 --- 1 季節有時被稱為蘇摩的兄弟，參見希勒布蘭特著，第 298 頁。
卷二 R 668 特里塔與特比塔 [章]
形式下降，蘇摩（Soma）乃為強大的因陀羅而備製。」特瓦什特里本身亦被稱為毗濕瓦盧波（visvarupa）（《梨俱吠陀》第三卷，55，19），據言其創造了一切形式與一切事物。

如果月亮或毘濕伐盧波（Visvarupa）掌握著蘇摩、雨水或夜晚沁涼的露水，那麼特里陀（Trita）或任何其他太陽神為了獲得振奮人心的蘇摩，與之交戰或砍下他的三個頭顱就完全是自然而然的事。這樣一來，我們對三首毘濕伐盧波、特瓦什特里（Tv&shtri）之子遭特里陀或因陀羅摧毀這一事件有了某種含義的理解。無論這是否正是梨俱吠陀的詩人或婆羅門書（Brahmawas）的作者心中的確切含義，我不敢斷言，但至少這是另一位學者、希勒布蘭特（Hillebrandt）教授在其《吠陀神話學》（Vedische Mythologie）第五十三頁及以後的篇幅中獨立得出的含義。迄今為止未有更好的詮釋被提出，在此之前，這種解釋是值得接受的。
因此，倘若吾人在 Trita 中看到源自單一根源 tar（通過之義）的衍生詞，其年代早於 tri，且該天體由東向西通過的觀念在吠陀中得到恆久的表現，問題乃是：它是否也在希臘神話中留下若干痕跡。
我們看到特里塔（Trita）據稱住在最遙遠的地區，那是太陽或日馬升起之處。就實質而言，這就是希臘人所謂的塔爾塔羅斯（Tartaros）的地方。然而，塔爾塔羅斯最初如同哈得斯（Hades）一樣，並非¹ ¹ Yad ardram tat saumyam, vatrih saumya. Taitt. Samh. I, 6, 3, 23. Hillebrandt, p. 459.
特尤斯和蓋亞之子的名字，他本身是堤豐、厄喀德那和巨人族的父親。荷馬總是將哈得斯用作人格名詞，而非地點名詞。我們可以在特里塔的例子中看到相同的轉變，因為雖然特里塔原本明顯是一個人物，但在某些篇章中，我們可以看到特里塔也呈現出地方性的特徵。在《梨俱吠陀》第九卷第37頌第4句中，我們讀到：*Sā Tritásya ddhí sanavi*，「（他點亮了太陽）在特里塔的頂峰上」。
塔爾塔羅（Tartara）是產生特里塔（Trita）之同一詞根的重疊形式，或毋寧說，特里塔（我們在希臘文中預期為塔普羅斯（Tapros））的形式，原義為「不斷越過者」或「在海中或在設置者」，或在夜間太陽沒落後被認為居住之處——亞摩（Yama）之地，亞摩本身原為夜間的太陽，有時被稱為首位死者。
班費已證明，塔爾塔洛斯（Tartaros）是塔刺塔刺（Talatala）的平行形式，此詞在梵文中指下界，但他將其理解為廣義的下方、下降或底部（tala）。他消除了所有關於 ταρταρος = talatala 這個等式的形式上的反對意見，但他並未察覺到特里塔（Trita）與 ταρταρος 之間的神話學關聯。「三」的強調式為 tatariti 與 tatarti。

既然我已經澄清了特里塔（Trita）的含義及其與下界、水中（samudre）或水下世界的密切關係，我現在比往日更傾向於接受特里塔（阿普堤亞）=特里同（Triton）這個等式。當然，特里同中的希臘文 i 是長音，因此這個等式在形式上是不規則的，但特里塔（Trita）從三（tri）衍生而來也是不規則的，而特里塔從「三」（tri）衍生也同樣如此，然而兩者都¹ ¹ 赫爾墨斯、米諾斯與塔爾塔羅斯，1877年。R 2 670 希臘神話中的特里塔。

是真實的形式。如果梵文中的不規則性是源於試圖為 TWta 提供更清晰的意義，那麼希臘文中 Triton 的長音 i 是否也可能源於 Tritogeneia 中 i 的長化呢？這是一個非常常見且流行的名字，如果 i 曾經在其中被長化，以便在史詩詩歌中使成為可能的名字，那麼說 Triton——即 TWta 的雙重訛變——幾乎就成了一種必然。因此，TWta 變為 Trita 的變化似乎不僅先於印歐語族，甚至先於雅利安分化。Triton 中的衍生詞綴與 Ploutos 和 Plouton 的詞綴不同，但若 Triton 是波塞冬與安菲特里忒（或凱萊尼歐）之子，若他與其他的特里同人（Tritons）與其父一起住在海底，這便與真實的 Trita 相符，甚至有助於我們理解為何同一個名字會在希臘的不同地方出現，也就是說，在太陽似乎下降之處，以及黑暗的環境曾暗示進入地下幽冥之處。因此，雅典娜的名字——如 Tritogeneia、Trito、Tritonia 和 Tritonis——可能會獲得新的闡釋，因為若吠陀中的太陽被設想為從 TWta 的頂端升起，那麼黎明女神便無法聲稱擁有更真實的出生地。

我們幾乎無須援引關於特里托革涅亞（Tritogeneia）的古老語源推導，無論是源自克里特語詞彙 (κρυά) 據稱意為「頭顱」，或源自波奧蒂亞河流特里同河，其流入靠近佩拉斯基亞城邦雅典娜城（Athenai）的科帕伊斯湖。兩種推導方式都會賦予特里托革涅亞這一名稱過於局限與狹隘的特徵，且都留下學院式解釋之印象，試圖解釋一個已變得不可理解的名稱。在後代，凡是有特里同河之處，民眾自然而然宣稱特里托尼亞（Tritonia）或特里托革涅亞乃其女神。

還有一步值得注意。我們已看到摩奴‧外婆斯瓦塔（Manu Vaivasvata）在梨俱吠陀中與特里塔（Trita, Rv. VIII, 52, 1）以及亡靈之主閻摩（Yama）一同被提及。在庫恩（Kuhn）與其後班費（Benfey）的解釋之後，摩奴乃梵文形式而與希臘文中的米諾斯相對應，這一點已不容置疑。於此，我們遂得出一項重要結論，即在雅利安民族分化發生之前，不僅已形成了對於冥界的信仰，而且至少已有一位人物被命名專司其事——在印度人祖先中為摩奴，在希臘人祖先中為米諾斯。

我曾經在某個時期提出，第三位神祇、特里塔（Trita）的觀念在日耳曼語族與印度雅利安語族分離之前就已經形成。我認為這可以根據古北歐語的 Thridi（奧丁神的一個名號，與 Har 和 Jafnhar 相對應）的證據而被接受。然而，格林的 Tweggi ¹ 則非常可疑，不足以代表 Dvita。若我們接受梵文中的 Trita 作為 TWta 的誤解，我們就必須承認這種誤解發生在雅利安分離之前。但是，把三位人物作為太陽在早晨、中午和夜晚的代表這一觀念，是如此自然，以至於我們不能否認它在雅利安語族的不同分支中獨立產生的可能性。另一方面，我們幾乎不能懷疑這種誤解發生在 ¹ Hermes, Minos und Tartaros, pp. 11-14.
2 德文《神話學》，第149頁。
672 希臘神話中的特里塔 [章]
印度和波斯雅利安人的分離，因為阿維斯陀文的 Thraetaona 預設了一個源自 Trita 的形式，如 Traitana（梨俱吠陀 I, 158, 5），而非直接源自 Trita。此外，在阿維斯陀中，Thrita 始終被稱作第三位蘇摩祭司（Soma-priests），被譽為最有益的蘇摩祭者，是為凡人世界製備蘇摩的第三位凡人，等等。

比較神話學中最為傑出的發現，恐怕莫過於以下這一點：波斯史詩《沙赫納美》的偉大英雄費里頓（Feridun）之名，係直接繼承自梵文的 Trita 和 Traitana，而吠陀中的 Trita Aptya 在《阿維斯陀》中作為 Thraetaona（阿蘇亞之子）出現。令人驚訝的是，這項布爾諾夫（Burnouf）的發現一次又一次地被誤歸為羅特（Roth）之功，而我相信羅特本人必定會首先對此提出異議。Vivasvat 與 Vivahvat 的同一性乃由博普（Bopp）所發現，然而正是布爾諾夫追蹤波斯的費里頓溯源至 Thraetaona，並將波斯的格爾沙斯卜（Gershasb）、《阿維斯陀》的 Keresaspa，追溯至吠陀的 Ksasva。自 1840 年至 1846 年，布爾諾夫在《亞洲雜誌》發表了其《瑣羅亞斯德語言及文本研究》（'Etudes sur la Langue et les Textes Zends'），首次展示波斯三位史詩英雄——耶姆希德、費里頓及格爾沙斯卜——如何分別源於 Yima Kshaeta、Thraetaona 及 Keresaspa，即這三位人類最早世代的《阿維斯陀》代表人物，而其更為遠古的原型則可在吠陀的 Yama、Trita 及 Ksasva 中得以發現。他進而證明，正如 Vivasvat 在吠陀中為 Yama 之父，Vivahvat 在《阿維斯陀》中亦為 Yima 之父；正如 Trita 在吠陀中被稱為 Aptya，Thraetaona 在波斯亦為 Athwya 之子。被費里頓所誅的波斯暴君 Zohak，同樣被追溯至《阿維斯陀》中的 Azhi dahaka（蛇魔），即吠陀的 Ahi。這些傑出的發現之所以重要，不僅在於它們揭示了吠陀、《阿維斯陀》與《沙赫納美》（西元一千年）之間的密切關係，更在於它們對古代神話向史詩詩歌，乃至所謂歷史的轉變過程提供了確實可見的例證——這一轉變過程長期遭人輕率地駁斥為不可信，當時有人試圖證明特洛伊之戰乃是神話事件而非嚴格意義上的歷史事件。
赫爾墨斯與阿波羅
如果我們認可宙斯代表天空之神在其最高榮耀中的形象，作為諸神的 *victtor* /*xeyicrros*；如果我們將大地——三位克洛諾斯之子的共同領域，主要由得墨忒耳、克瑞、赫斯提亞及其他女性力量所代表——撇在一旁，我們仍然必須分析其他一些提婆，代表太陽在其升起與降落之時，或作為朝暮之空的形象，即赫耳墨斯與阿波羅。
我們已經考察過黎明女神的多個名稱；但還有一個極其重要的名稱，其重要性不在於黎明女神本身，而在於她的兒子。
赫爾墨斯‧薩拉瑪。我們已經看到，薩拉瑪如同薩蘭尤一樣，本來是指晨曦，其名字的意思是「迅速的奔跑者」。這對晨曦而言似乎是個毫無意義的名字。但我們現在已經知道，幾乎所有的名字在其源始之時都是非常通俗的，在這個意義上是無內涵的。這是無法避免的。因此從詞根 sar（去、跑）出發，我們得出……
不僅有薩羅摩（Sarama）這個「迅速行者」，而且還有薩羅（sara）意為「瀑布」，薩羅摩（sarama）意為「風」，薩羅斯（saras）意為「水」，薩里特（sarit）意為「河流」，薩羅尤（sarayu）意為「風」、「空氣」，薩里（sari）意為「泉源」，薩爾摩（sarma）意為「運動」。所有這些詞在最初都是通用的，但卻因持續的使用而逐漸被限定下來，變成了特殊的詞彙。

赫爾墨斯這個名字，然而，並非直接源自根詞 sar，而是源自該根詞的衍生詞，即薩拉瑪（Sarama）。我們在檢視「二與一」的許多故事時已經看到，她的名字隨著神話的流傳而被保留下來，先是作為狄奧斯庫羅伊（Dioskouroi）的妹妹，後來則作為特洛伊的海倫。我們不應該想像《吠陀》中的薩拉瑪與荷馬筆下的海倫娜是同一個人。那樣就說不通了。我們唯一有權說的是，她們都源自薩拉瑪，在《吠陀》中用作晨曦女神的名字，或來自更古老的事物。但之後，當許多關於晨曦的故事被講述時，希臘人和印度人的道路從此分道揚鑣。薩拉瑪雖然成為了因陀羅與帕尼之間戰爭的起因，卻從未成為荷馬所述的那位美麗女性。海倫娜一旦被改變成宙斯和勒達的女兒、墨涅勞斯的妻子以及特洛伊戰爭的起因，就不再顯示出她晨曦或犬類起源的任何痕跡。在希臘，我們發現不止一位海倫娜，因為當她被忒修斯擄走時和被帕里斯擄走時，她難以被稱為同一位神話人物。所有這些故事，乃至可能更多的故事，都是由阿爾戈斯人民所述說的，它們共同之處無非是遙遠的物理背景——晨曦被無數求婚者所愛慕並被擄走，以及在東方與西方、光明與黑暗的力量之間進行了一場漫長的戰鬥後被重新征服。

在《吠陀》中，薩拉瑪（Sarama）被提及為一隻犬（然而不是作為圖騰），而是作為一隻迅捷的犬沿著天空奔馳，據說能尋得那些被帕尼人（Paṇis）掠走並藏匿於洞穴中的牛群。關於烏莎絲（Ushas）所說的事，也同樣被說到薩拉瑪身上：她窺見了牛的牢固畜棚，她發現了岩石的裂隙，她帶領牛群走了出來。然而她被描繪為做這一切的並非出於自己的自由意志，而是作為因陀羅（Indra）的使者。在希臘神話中，我們聽聞有一隻類似的犬，曾屬於厄里戈涅（Erigone，早生者），然而關於那隻被稱為邁拉（Maira）的犬，所知甚少，而它要尋找的並非牛群，而是厄里戈涅的父親伊卡洛斯（Ikarios）的屍體，無論那個傳說本來想要表達什麼。
吠陀的故事述說著一個在半真實、半神話的拉薩河（Rasa）彼岸的馬廄。莎羅摩（Sarama）必須渡過那條河才能到達母牛。馬廄終於被因陀羅（Indra）或婆羅訶斯帕提（Bṛhaspati）打開，而母牛，也就是晨光的光線，因此得到釋放。這只是晨間母牛的古老故事以新的形式再次講述，在我們前面所見過的其各種版本之後，目前無需詳細論述。
薩拉梅雅烏（Sarameyau）。
目前引起我們注意的是，薩拉瑪（Sarama）據說是一隻或兩隻狗的母親，這些狗被稱為薩拉美亞（Sarameya）。這項發現是庫恩的功勞。1 1 奧夫雷希特，載《Z. D. M. G.》第十三卷，第493頁。
